content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.6.1/5.6.3 WARN — RatingGateView.swift gates the App Store write-review link behind a custom star-rating UI, only routing users who tap 4-5 stars to `itms-apps://...write-review`, while diverting lower ratings to a feedback form (App/RatingGateView.swift:12-21); metadata also solicits "rate us 5 stars!" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:1), and no `SKStoreReviewController`/`requestReview` API usage is found anywhere in the project.
Pierre: Apple explicitly disallows custom review prompts and requires use of the system `requestReview`/`SKStoreReviewController` API, which shows Apple's native prompt to all users regardless of sentiment — filtering users by star rating before sending only happy ones to the App Store is a textbook rating-gate dark pattern under 5.6.1, and it's compounded by review-manipulation copy in the description ("rate us 5 stars"). Fix by removing the pre-filter and custom star UI, replacing the write-review deep link with `SKStoreReviewController.requestReview(in:)` (or `RequestReviewAction` in SwiftUI), and dropping the "rate us 5 stars" language from metadata.
